evals.prompt: Write only a proposed W3C Message Resource (.mfr) for Acme Inbox. Include a resource-level translator comment, the frontmatter marker, a fully qualified `inbox.notifications` section with a section comment, a static `title` entry, and a `count` entry that selects singular/plural text from an external integer $count. Add translator-facing parameter metadata for $count.
evals.expected_output: A valid .mfr resource with correctly placed comments and frontmatter, a full section identifier, static and complex MF2 entries, parameter metadata, a typed declaration, and a catch-all variant.
assertions: [standard] The resource-level comment and metadata/frontmatter ordering satisfy the Message Resource attachment and placement rules.
[standard] The section uses the full identifier inbox.notifications and contains valid title and count entries.
[standard] Every continuation line of the complex count value is indented, and the embedded value is a valid MF2 message with an all-* fallback.
[convention] The section comment supplies translator context and @param metadata explains $count before the count entry.
[convention] The external $count input is explicitly annotated with :integer.

evals.prompt:
Review this proposed Message Resource. Identify each resource-container error separately from any embedded MF2 error, then return a corrected resource.

```mfr
welcome = Hello
---
@owner localization

# Settings strings.
[settings]
bad?key = Oops
items =
.input {$count :integer}
.match $count
one {{One item}}
* {{Many items}}
```
evals.expected_output: A diagnosis covering misplaced frontmatter, detached metadata, an unescaped question mark in an identifier, and unindented multiline value lines, plus a corrected valid resource whose embedded MF2 remains valid.
assertions: [standard] The response identifies that frontmatter cannot follow an entry and either moves it before all entries and section headers or removes the empty frontmatter marker.

[standard] The response explains that the blank line prevents @owner from attaching to the following comment or section under the proposal's attachment semantics, then places it with an intended target or removes it; it does not incorrectly call the metadata line itself an ABNF violation.
[standard] The response identifies and fixes the unescaped question mark in bad?key while preserving the key's literal identity, for example as bad\x3fkey.
[standard] The response identifies and fixes every unindented continuation line under items.
[standard] The response distinguishes resource-container failures from the embedded MF2 message, which is otherwise valid.

evals.prompt:
Migrate these source messages into only a valid proposed `.mfr` resource. Preserve meaning and significant whitespace. Use translator context where it helps.

- `plain.title`: `Welcome`
- `items`: ICU MessageFormat 1 `{count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}}`, with integer count
- `separator`: a comma followed by exactly one trailing space
- literal source key `Are you sure?`: `Continue?`
evals.expected_output: A valid .mfr resource with a structural plain.title identifier, an MF2 matcher for items, a separator that resolves to comma-plus-space without literal vertical bars, and an escaped identifier for the question-mark key.
assertions: [standard] The output is a grammatically valid Message Resource and every entry value is valid MF2.
[standard] plain.title uses a structural dot, while the literal question mark in the source key is escaped in the resource identifier.
[standard] items is migrated to an MF2 matcher with a declared selector and an all-* fallback while preserving singular/plural meaning.
[standard] separator resolves to exactly comma plus one trailing space; vertical bars are not introduced as literal output characters.
[convention] Translator context and parameter metadata are useful but do not obscure the resource syntax.
